headline: Water Privatisation in Selangor Costs Consumers RM1.4B Extra Annually
context: The restructured Selangor water concession under Air Selangor resulted in tariff increases totalling RM1.4 billion in additional annual consumer costs since 2019, while NRW (non-revenue water) remained at 28% due to ageing pipe infrastructure.

cards.big: Selangor water tariffs up RM1.4 billion yearly — but 28% of treated water leaks from pipes before reaching taps
cards.sub: Consumers pay more for water while 690 million litres per day are lost to leaking infrastructure built 40 years ago

cards.big: NRW rate of 28% means RM890 million worth of treated water wasted annually — enough to supply 1.2 million households
cards.sub: Pipe replacement programme at current RM400 million yearly budget will take 48 years to complete the full network

cards.big: 8.2 million Selangor residents experienced water disruption averaging 4 days annually in 2025
cards.sub: Low-income flats without water tanks are worst affected — B40 families spend RM180 annually buying emergency bottled water

cards.big: Air Selangor's guaranteed ROA of 7.5% means profits are secured even when service quality declines
cards.sub: Consumer complaints rose 42% in 2025 but tariff review mechanism has no service quality linkage

cards.big: Guaranteeing water company profits while 28% of water leaks from their pipes rewards failure — not performance
Should Air Selangor's guaranteed returns be reduced by 1% for every percentage point NRW exceeds 20%?
